techmeme-2022.text: Leaked audio from 80+ internal TikTok meetings: ByteDance staff in China repeatedly accessed data about US TikTok users between September 2021 and January 2022

TikTok moves U.S. user data to Oracle servers
It looks like China did have access to U.S. TikTok user data

TikTok announces it's now storing US user data on Oracle cloud servers and that “100% of US user traffic” is being hosted by Oracle. Backups are still stored on TikTok's own servers in the US and Singapore for now, but those will eventually be deleted: https://newsroom.tiktok.com/ ...

In the past week, I listened to recordings of more than 80 internal TikTok meetings about Project Texas, the company's effort to restrict access to US user data. TLDR: TikTok has been having a really hard time shutting down Chinese access to this info. https://www.buzzfeednews.com/ ...

“Ultimately, the tapes suggest that the company may have misled lawmakers, its users, and the public by downplaying that data stored in the US could still be accessed by employees in China.” https://www.buzzfeednews.com/ ...

This is definitely a Bad Thing, but it's bizarre we make a big deal only of this when big tech has so much of our data, actively uses it against us, and once an authoritarian government is in control will likely be used to target us in worse ways than China ever could. https://twitter.com/...

The article is better than the headline. As I see it, folks reading this as 'Trump was right", are seriously misunderstanding the concerns surrounding the proposed tiktok ban. ... https://twitter.com/...

In line with our ongoing commitment to further safeguard user data, minimize data transfers and enhance personal data protections, we recently created a new in-house department to manage TikTok's US user data. Learn more: https://newsroom.tiktok.com/ ...

Whoops: “Everything is seen in China,” said a member of TikTok's Trust and Safety department in a September 2021 meeting. In another September meeting, a director referred to one Beijing-based engineer as a “Master Admin” who “has access to everything.” https://twitter.com/...

Leaked audio from more than 80 internal TikTok meetings shows that China-based employees of ByteDance have repeatedly accessed nonpublic data about US TikTok users. “Everything is seen in China,” said a member of TikTok's Trust and Safety department in one Sept 2021 meeting. https://twitter.com/...

Some of you may have noticed that TikTok put up a blog post this morning about their US data practices! It was published just about exactly at the deadline we had given them for comment for this story. We will certainly have some follow-up questions. https://www.buzzfeednews.com/ ...
This happened because Trump demanded TikTok be sold. Even though TikTok wasn't sold. https://twitter.com/...
China has instrumented a hundreds ways to get data on US industry and consumers. Question is not: “are they doing it?” Question is: “what are we going to do about it?” https://twitter.com/...
Let's say you're someone who says “who cares” “nothing to hide” ok but do you care that these leaks indicate that the company behind TikTok is being dishonest? https://www.buzzfeednews.com/ ...

“according to leaked audio from 80+ internal TikTok meetings, China-based employees of ByteDance have repeatedly accessed nonpublic data about US TikTok users — exactly the type of behavior that inspired Trump to threaten to ban the app” https://www.buzzfeednews.com/ ... @ebakerwhite
This is unsurprising and there should be outrage in Congress about this report. TikTok has repeatedly said they do not share user data with China. They lied. https://twitter.com/...

“I feel like with these tools, there's some backdoor to access user data in almost all of them,” said an external auditor hired to help TikTok close off Chinese access to sensitive information https://twitter.com/...

BlockFi can confirm that we exercised our best business judgment recently with a large client that failed to meet its obligations on an overcollateralized margin loan. We fully accelerated the loan and fully liquidated or hedged all the associated collateral.

I've been seeing a lot of speculation about BlockFi's risk management practices that I want to address. While our policy is to not comment on specific counterparties, we are committed to providing as much transparency as possible, and it's important to know the following:

Elon Musk told Twitter employees in a live Q. and A. Thursday that the platform “should allow people to say what they want,” and he seemed preoccupied with people not seeing “boring” content. He also dodged a question about layoffs. Follow live updates. https://www.nytimes.com/...
somehow the discussion turned to aliens. Elon: “I have seen no actual evidence for aliens.”
Here's the entire thing. All of it. The whole reason the world's richest man is obsessed with a smallish and barely profitable social network. https://twitter.com/...
Elon also said he wants t get to 1 billion users for Twitter. That would be a goal for him if he acquires the company. The meeting is now over.

Shocked to find out a certain billionaire doesn't really have a plan, but he thinks companies should get “healthy,” whatever that means, and a whole lot more customers would be good. Thoughts and prayers to everyone at Twitter trying to do their job and feed their families.
Have people never seen Elon speak? Of course he's winging it. He always speaks off the cuff. https://twitter.com/...

and on that note, that's a wrap! till round two, which Musk agreed to do in the future. check the live blog for all the updates I and others added that were not in tweet form. https://www.nytimes.com/...

(PR) TSMC announces FINFLEX technology for its N3 node, nanosheet-based N2 in 2025
FinFlex video from TSMC here: https://n3.tsmc.com/...

2/5 TSMC 1. N2 will begin production in 2025 and offer 10-15% speed improvement, or 25-30% power reduction. 2. N2 will be first TSMC node to use GAAFETs, nanosheet-based Gate-All-Around Field-Effect Transistors, a costly change for clients, in time, new design techniques, IP.

TSMC discloses their N2 node for 2026 products will feature >1.1x chip density improvement over N3E (50% Logic, 30% SRAM, 20% Analog). Transistor Area Scaling grinds to a crawl! https://twitter.com/...
9/5 TSMC Best article and source for most of these tweets: https://www.anandtech.com/...

Delivering a major update to their roadmap, TSMC today has revealed that they are developing a total of 5 versions of their N3 process. Joining N3 will be N3E, N3P, N3S, and N3X, giving more design options, as well as FinFlex tech for low-level flexibility https://www.anandtech.com/... https://twitter.com/...

4/5 TSMC will expand production capacity for mature and specialty processes 50% by 2025 1. Japan: Kumamoto 300mm plant. Processes: N12, N16, N22, and N28. Up to 45,000 wafer starts per month. 2. Taiwan: Fab 14 in Tainan, Fab 22 in Kaohsiung. 3. China: Nanjing N28 $GFS $UMC $TSM
This is the chip inside Apple's AR/VR headset. The Big Bang approaches! https://twitter.com/...

As TSMC's last “classic” FinFET node, the N3 family is going to be around for quite a while. As a result, the company is preparing a further 4 versions of the node, for a total of 5 variants of N3 https://twitter.com/...

This is ironic from a champion of “free speech.” I guess the takeaway here is when free speech becomes distracting because it's harassing, time-wasting or abusive, it should be limited, do I have that right? https://twitter.com/...

I was asked by a journalist to comment on Twitter and Musk's plans for it. I declined to comment. Given his army of trolls who go after anyone who is critical of him my thought was, it's not worth the hassle of Elon's Twitter Mob or being called a pedophile.
